next_steps: Keep the entities separate and relation:08 tentative.
Seek Lambeth historical shelfmark evidence before asserting identity.
openQuestions.question: Is Furnivall’s MS 583 citation a transposition for MS 853?

next_steps: Verify microfilm inventory and completeness before proposing new photography.
openQuestions.question: Do MS Film 1232 or World Microfilms Section I Reel 15 cover all of MS 853, and can one be supplied or digitized?

next_steps: After authorization, ask EETS, Trinity, or relevant archives; do not infer destruction from present non-discovery.
openQuestions.question: Do the three Wright working transcripts survive, and where are they held?

next_steps: Establish survival and publication history before any reuse conclusion.
openQuestions.question: Were Wright’s transcripts ever published, and does UK transitional protection through 2039 apply?

next_steps: Keep the software-worded Unlicense scope narrow pending provenance evidence.
openQuestions.question: What rights did the CME repository affirmer hold and intend to waive in inherited U-M/XML/edition content?

next_steps: Preserve both statements and ask U-M for item-level production history.
openQuestions.question: How does the item’s OCR/no-correction header relate to the collection page’s manual-keying/proofreading description?

next_steps: Use ordinary public routes only; do not bypass the scripted-route 403 response.
openQuestions.question: Is a complete public package of the 164 linked TIFFs stated in the U-M header available?

next_steps: Retain Relief as an encoded OCR variant and Belief as the preferred part title regardless of upstream correction.
openQuestions.question: Will U-M/CME correct XML ‘The Relief’ to printed and cataloged ‘The Belief’?

next_steps: Keep them as null-count analytical paratext unless exact work-identity evidence establishes otherwise.
openQuestions.question: Are the five Latin lines before The Belief a separately circulating work?

reviewNotes: The four complete public manuscript IIIF bundles cover every provider-exposed canvas at the recorded request profile; only target work portions are cataloged.
The thirteen Lambeth objects are complete one-canvas provider objects but partial coverage of MS 853.
No direct edition-to-Trinity witness relation is asserted because Furnivall explicitly identifies Wright transcript intermediaries.
Every rights record joins an access record for the same entity; modern images, provider terms, edition text, and repository-license scope remain separate layers.

lineage.summary: The pinned repository XML copies U-M CME item ANT9911.0001.001, an uncorrected-OCR encoding of the 1895 reprint title leaf and printed pp. 1–128 from Furnivall’s twenty-six-item 1867 selection. The 1895 reprint is modeled separately from the 1867 edition; the edition transcribes Lambeth MS 853 and one Vernon stream, while Wright’s three pre-1867 transcripts mediate the final Trinity sources. MS 583 remains an unresolved historical citation, separate from MS 853. The graph contains 24 entities, 31 relations, 27 access records, 28 rights records, 29 evidence records, and 9 open questions; 42 access-linked local copies sit within a 47-file fixity-verified cache projection.

catalogingSubject.label: Hymns to the Virgin and Christ, the Parliament of Devils, and Other Religious Poems (Furnivall twenty-six-item selection)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The exact edition-defined Furnivall selection encoded by ANT9911.0001.001: the 1895 reprint title leaf and printed pp. 1–128, comprising three Virgin hymns, eight Christ-centered items, and fifteen other religious items. It is not one medieval work, the whole of Lambeth MS 853, every text Furnivall mentions, or the whole 136-page print carrier; printed pp. 129–136 and all other front/back matter are excluded.

extent.description: Thirty-three content-part records comprise 26 count-bearing intellectual items, three zero-count structural groups, one zero-count editorial-apparatus frame, two null-count analytical Kepe Wel version streams, and one null-count analytical Latin preface. The count-once aggregate is 3,360 lines/392 groups; the full two-stream representation is 3,464 lines/405 groups and appears only here and in supporting evidence.

formStatements.description: Twenty-five verse works and one prose work.
The one count-bearing prose work; XML L/LG layout does not make it verse.

scope.description: The count-bearing medieval items other than the scoped Caistre attribution
Selection and arrangement of the twenty-six-item edition-defined unit

notes: Wright supplied nineteenth-century transcripts for Furnivall; he is not assigned medieval authorship.

summaries.value: Furnivall’s bounded edition-defined selection brings together twenty-six anonymous or attributed medieval religious items: three hymns to the Virgin, eight Christ-centered items, and fifteen further devotional, moral, eschatological, lament, and historical pieces. Twenty-five are verse and one is prose; Kepe Wel Cristes Comaundement appears in two parallel witness versions but counts once as one intellectual-work family.

Encoded boundary: 1895 reprint title leaf and printed pp. 1–128. Excluded: all other front matter and printed pp. 129–136 (Halle extract, glossary, and index). Modern headings, ARGUMENT, associated prose, and NOTE1 material inside the selected pages are modeled as zero-count editorial apparatus.

Count-once intellectual extent is 3,360 verse lines in 392 groups. Full two-stream serialization/representation is 3,464 verse lines in 405 groups; the additional 104 lines and 13 groups are the second complete Kepe Wel version stream and are retained in null-count analytical children, not re-added to the abstract-work total.

assertion.notes: The supplied qualifier preserves the edition-defined twenty-six-item boundary.
